major: History of Art and Visual Culture Minor

groups.group_label: Complete 3 semester units from the following

courses.title: Colonial Histories and Legacies: Africa, Oceania, and the Indigenous Americas
History of Art: Arts of Africa, Oceania and Native North America
History of Art: Arts of Africa, Oceania and Native North America

notes: UC Santa Cruz students may complete one or more minors, provided they can finish all required coursework within their maximum enrollment terms and credit limits. Minors are recorded on the official transcript but do not appear on the diploma. Pursuing a minor is optional and should align with a student’s academic interests and career goals. Students do not apply for minors as part of their UC Santa Cruz admission application. Transfer students interested in a minor should reach out to the department that offers it for advising during their first quarter of enrollment.

The History of Art and Visual Culture (HAVC) program at UC Santa Cruz welcomes transfer students. For the minor, a student may transfer up to three lower-division art history courses taken from other institutions, each satisfying a different geographic regional requirement: Africa and its Diaspora Asia and its Diaspora Europe and the Americas Mediterranean Native Americas Oceania and its Diaspora HAVC 80 – Colonial Histories and Legacies: Africa, Oceania, and the Indigenous Americas* *HAVC 80, or an articulated equivalent, may be used to fulfill a lower-division requirement for one of the following geographic regions: Africa, Native Americas, or Oceania. In the “Preparation for the Minor” section below, you will find lists of courses approved to fulfill these requirements. Petitioning Courses: Transfer students who have completed courses they believe are applicable to the geographic regions listed above, but are not listed in the "Recommended Preparation" section below, may petition to have these courses counted toward their HAVC minor requirements. Students should consult the Transfer Credit Petition Process instructions on the HAVC Department website . Please visit the History of Art and Visual Culture website to learn more about this minor. UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: History of Art and Visual Culture Minor Program Statement
